docs.text:
uname -a
systemctl --failed

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Linux Server Baseline Standard

docs.title: Ubuntu 26.04 AI 全栈开发环境配置手册 v2.0
适用范围与约束声明
docs.text:
适用： - OS：Ubuntu 26.04 LTS Desktop（GNOME，X11 或 Wayland） - 硬件：x86_64，无 GPU，8GB+ RAM - 用途：个人 AI 应用开发工作站（Go + Python + Vue.js + Claude API + 向量数据库） - 执行身份：具备 sudo 权限的普通用户

不适用： - 服务器 / headless 环境 - NVIDIA GPU 场景（不覆盖 CUDA 安装） - 生产部署（本手册仅覆盖本地开发工作流） - 多人共享机器（secrets 管理策略需调整）

风险声明： - Claude Desktop Linux 版为社区非官方构建，Anthropic 不提供支持 - Ollama 在纯 CPU 环境下推理速度有限，仅适合开发期流程验证 - 本手册涉及修改 shell 配置、添加第三方 APT 仓库，建议在全新安装的系统上执行

预计耗时： 2.5–4 小时（含下载时间）

docs.title: 架构总览
docs.text:
开发工作站组件栈
├── 系统层
│   ├── Ubuntu 26.04 基础配置（禁休眠、中文输入、Swap）
│   └── Shell 环境（Bash + direnv + 统一 env 文件）
├── 工具层
│   ├── Git + SSH + 全局 .gitignore
│   ├── VSCode（官方 .deb）
│   └── tmux（终端复用）
├── 语言层
│   ├── Go（官方 tarball）
│   ├── Python（pyenv + uv）
│   └── Node.js（nvm + pnpm）
├── 容器层
│   └── Docker Engine + Compose（统一 docker-compose 管理本地服务）
├── 服务层（docker-compose 统一编排）
│   ├── Qdrant（向量数据库）
│   ├── PostgreSQL + pgvector（关系型 + 向量）
│   ├── Redis（缓存 / 会话 / 队列）
│   └── Langfuse（LLM 可观测性）
├── AI 客户端层
│   ├── Claude Code CLI（官方 Linux 路径）
│   ├── Claude Desktop（非官方 .deb，可选）
│   └── Ollama（本地 LLM，开发期成本控制）
└── 项目模板层
    ├── Makefile（工作流标准化）
    ├── .envrc（per-project 环境变量）
    └── docker-compose.yml（本地服务编排）

核心设计原则： - Shell 环境变量单一来源：~/.config/devenv/env.sh，~/.bashrc 只 source 一次 - 本地服务统一 docker-compose 管理，不直接 docker run - 开发期用 Ollama 验证流程，仅在集成测试和生产调用 Claude API，控制成本 - per-project 环境变量用 direnv 隔离，不污染全局

docs.title: Phase 0：预检
docs.text:
执行所有后续步骤前，先确认环境满足基础条件。

# 确认系统版本
lsb_release -a

# 确认是 GNOME 桌面
echo $XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP

# 检查可用磁盘空间（建议 /home 至少剩余 40GB）
df -h ~

# 检查内存
free -h

# 确认 sudo 可用
sudo whoami    # 应返回 root

# 确认网络（如需代理请提前配置）
curl -fsSL https://go.dev/VERSION?m=text

继续前提： 磁盘剩余 ≥ 40GB，RAM ≥ 8GB，网络可访问境外资源。

docs.title: 1.1 系统更新与基础工具
docs.text:
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

sudo apt install -y \
  curl wget git build-essential \
  ca-certificates gnupg lsb-release \
  software-properties-common apt-transport-https \
  unzip zip jq tree htop net-tools \
  tmux xclip libfuse2 imagemagick p7zip-full icoutils

验证： 

git --version && curl --version && tmux -V

docs.title: 1.2 Swap 配置
docs.text:
为什么： 纯 CPU 运行 Embedding 模型或 Ollama 推理时，内存压力显著。没有 Swap 会触发 OOM Killer，进程被强制终止。

# 检查当前 Swap 状态
free -h && swapon --show

# 如果没有 Swap，创建 8GB Swapfile
if ! swapon --show | grep -q .; then
  sudo fallocate -l 8G /swapfile
  sudo chmod 600 /swapfile
  sudo mkswap /swapfile
  sudo swapon /swapfile
  echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab
  echo "Swapfile created"
fi

# 降低 swappiness（开发机不应频繁使用 Swap，只作保底）
echo 'vm.swappiness=10' | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-swappiness.conf
sudo sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-swappiness.conf

验证： 

free -h    # SWAP 行应显示 8G 可用
cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness    # 应返回 10

docs.title: 1.3 禁用休眠与息屏
docs.text:
# systemd 层屏蔽所有休眠目标
sudo systemctl mask sleep.target suspend.target hibernate.target hybrid-sleep.target

# GNOME 层关闭息屏与自动挂起
gsettings set org.gnome.settings-daemon.plugins.power sleep-inactive-ac-type 'nothing'
gsettings set org.gnome.settings-daemon.plugins.power sleep-inactive-battery-type 'nothing'
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-enabled false
gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false

验证： 

systemctl is-enabled sleep.target    # 应返回 masked
gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay    # 应返回 uint32 0

回滚（如需恢复休眠）： 

sudo systemctl unmask sleep.target suspend.target hibernate.target hybrid-sleep.target

docs.title: 1.4 中文输入法（Fcitx5）
docs.text:
为什么选 Fcitx5 而不是 IBus： Ubuntu 22.04 起 Fcitx5 已是 Linux 桌面首选输入框架，与 Wayland 兼容更好，中文扩展（拼音、五笔、Rime）更完整，社区维护更活跃。IBus 在 GNOME Wayland 下有已知的光标跟随问题。

sudo apt install -y \
  fcitx5 \
  fcitx5-chinese-addons \
  fcitx5-frontend-gtk4 \
  fcitx5-frontend-qt5 \
  im-config

# 设置为系统默认输入法框架
im-config -n fcitx5

# 设置开机自启
mkdir -p ~/.config/autostart
cat > ~/.config/autostart/fcitx5.desktop << 'EOF'
[Desktop Entry]
Name=Fcitx5
Exec=fcitx5
Terminal=false
Type=Application
Categories=System;Utility;
StartupNotify=false
EOF

注销并重新登录后： 

fcitx5 &
fcitx5-configtool    # 在 Input Method 中添加 Pinyin（拼音）

VSCode 中文输入问题（已知问题）：

VSCode 从应用菜单启动时不继承 Fcitx5 环境变量，导致无法输入中文。解决方法：

# 创建 VSCode 启动脚本，从终端启动以继承环境
cat > ~/.local/bin/code-fcitx << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
export GTK_IM_MODULE=fcitx
export QT_IM_MODULE=fcitx
export XMODIFIERS=@im=fcitx
exec code "$@"
EOF
chmod +x ~/.local/bin/code-fcitx

# 修改桌面快捷方式使用该脚本
sudo cp /usr/share/applications/code.desktop ~/.local/share/applications/
sed -i 's|Exec=/usr/share/code/code|Exec=/home/'"$USER"'/.local/bin/code-fcitx|g' \
  ~/.local/share/applications/code.desktop

常见失败模式： - Wayland 下输入法不跟随光标：Fcitx5 → Behavior → 勾选 "Show preedit in application window" - 切换快捷键冲突：默认 Ctrl+Space，可在 Fcitx5 Config → Global Options 中修改

docs.title: 1.5 Nerd Fonts
docs.text:
为什么： VSCode 和终端（tmux、oh-my-posh 等）使用图标字体，未安装 Nerd Fonts 会显示方块乱码。

mkdir -p ~/.local/share/fonts/JetBrainsMono

# 下载 JetBrainsMono Nerd Font（推荐：等宽、连字支持好）
wget -q "https://github.com/ryanoasis/nerd-fonts/releases/download/v3.2.1/JetBrainsMono.zip" \
  -O /tmp/JetBrainsMono.zip

unzip -o /tmp/JetBrainsMono.zip \
  "JetBrainsMonoNerdFont-Regular.ttf" \
  "JetBrainsMonoNerdFont-Bold.ttf" \
  "JetBrainsMonoNerdFont-Italic.ttf" \
  -d ~/.local/share/fonts/JetBrainsMono/

fc-cache -fv
rm /tmp/JetBrainsMono.zip

VSCode 中启用： Settings → editor.fontFamily → 'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font', monospace

docs.title: 1.6 tmux（终端复用）
docs.text:
为什么： AI 开发同时需要运行：后端服务、前端 dev server、docker compose 日志、LLM 推理进程。tmux 允许在一个终端窗口管理多个会话和窗格，且 SSH 断连后进程不丢失。

# 基础 tmux 配置（鼠标支持 + 合理快捷键）
cat > ~/.tmux.conf << 'EOF'
# 前缀键改为 Ctrl+a（比默认 Ctrl+b 更顺手）
unbind C-b
set-option -g prefix C-a
bind-key C-a send-prefix

# 鼠标支持
set -g mouse on

# 窗口从 1 开始编号（与键盘布局一致）
set -g base-index 1
setw -g pane-base-index 1

# 快捷键：垂直 / 水平分割
bind | split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind - split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# 增大历史记录行数
set -g history-limit 10000

# 256 色支持
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
EOF

常用快捷键参考：

操作 快捷键 垂直分割 Ctrl+a | 水平分割 Ctrl+a - 切换窗格 Ctrl+a 方向键 新建窗口 Ctrl+a c 切换窗口 Ctrl+a 数字 分离会话 Ctrl+a d 重连会话 tmux attach

docs.title: Phase 2：Shell 环境架构
docs.text: 为什么单独作为一个 Phase： 后续所有工具都需要修改环境变量。如果每个工具各自追加到 ~/.bashrc，会产生重复条目、顺序错乱、幂等性失效等问题。本 Phase 建立统一的环境变量管理架构，后续工具全部写入这个统一文件。

docs.title: 2.1 建立统一环境变量文件
docs.text:
# 创建目录结构
mkdir -p ~/.config/devenv
mkdir -p ~/.config/dev-secrets

# 创建统一 env 文件（所有工具的 PATH 和 export 集中在此）
cat > ~/.config/devenv/env.sh << 'EOF'
# ============================================================
# Dev Environment - 统一管理，工具安装时追加到此文件
# ============================================================

# Go
export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin
export GOPATH=$HOME/go
export PATH=$PATH:$GOPATH/bin

# pyenv
export PYENV_ROOT="$HOME/.pyenv"
[[ -d $PYENV_ROOT/bin ]] && export PATH="$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH"
command -v pyenv > /dev/null && eval "$(pyenv init -)"

# uv
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# nvm（通过 nvm.sh 加载）
export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && source "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
[ -s "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion" ] && source "$NVM_DIR/bash_completion"

# direnv hook
command -v direnv > /dev/null && eval "$(direnv hook bash)"

# 本地 bin
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

# Secrets（API Keys 等，不纳入 Git）
[ -f "$HOME/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env" ] && source "$HOME/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env"
EOF

# ~/.bashrc 只添加一行 source（幂等，添加前检查是否已存在）
grep -q 'devenv/env.sh' ~/.bashrc \
  || echo '[ -f "$HOME/.config/devenv/env.sh" ] && source "$HOME/.config/devenv/env.sh"' >> ~/.bashrc

source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: 2.2 API Key 管理
docs.text:
# 创建 secrets 文件（权限 600，仅当前用户可读）
cat > ~/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env << 'EOF'
# Anthropic
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="<ANTHROPIC_API_KEY>"

# OpenAI（如需）
# export OPENAI_API_KEY="<OPENAI_API_KEY>"

# 本地服务（无需保密，但统一放这里方便项目引用）
export QDRANT_HOST="localhost"
export QDRANT_PORT="6333"
export REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379"
export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://devuser:<LOCAL_DEV_POSTGRES_PASSWORD>@localhost:5432/devdb"
EOF

chmod 600 ~/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env
source ~/.bashrc

验证： 

echo $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | cut -c1-10    # 显示 Key 前 10 位

docs.title: 2.3 direnv（per-project 环境变量）
docs.text:
为什么： 全局 secrets 文件适合通用 Key，但不同项目有不同的模型选择、不同的服务端口、不同的 DB 名。direnv 实现进入目录自动加载、离开自动卸载，互不污染。

sudo apt install -y direnv
# direnv hook 已在 env.sh 中配置，直接生效
source ~/.bashrc

每个项目的用法： 

cd ~/projects/my-ai-app

cat > .envrc << 'EOF'
# 项目专属变量（覆盖全局）
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="<PROJECT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY>"
export MODEL="claude-sonnet-4-6"
export QDRANT_COLLECTION="my-app-vectors"
export LOG_LEVEL="debug"
EOF

direnv allow .    # 首次允许加载
echo '.envrc' >> .gitignore    # 不提交 secrets

docs.title: 2.4 全局 .gitignore
docs.text:
git config --global core.excludesfile ~/.gitignore_global

cat > ~/.gitignore_global << 'EOF'
# Secrets
.env
.env.*
.envrc
*.key
*.pem
*.p12
*_rsa
*_ed25519

# Python
__pycache__/
*.py[cod]
*.pyo
.venv/
venv/
.pytest_cache/
*.egg-info/
dist/
build/

# Go
*.exe
*.test
vendor/

# Node.js
node_modules/
dist/
.nuxt/
.output/

# IDE
.idea/
.vscode/settings.json
*.swp
*.swo

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Logs & Data
*.log
*.sqlite
*.db
EOF

docs.title: Phase 3：Git 与 SSH 配置
docs.text:
# Git 基础配置
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"
git config --global init.defaultBranch main
git config --global pull.rebase false
git config --global push.autoSetupRemote true

# SSH Key 生成（如已有可跳过）
[ -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ] || ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "you@example.com" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

# SSH config 模板（多账户 / 多服务器场景）
mkdir -p ~/.ssh && chmod 700 ~/.ssh
cat > ~/.ssh/config << 'EOF'
# GitHub 主账户
Host github.com
  HostName github.com
  User git
  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
  AddKeysToAgent yes

# 开发服务器（按需填写）
# Host dev-server
#   HostName 192.168.1.xxx
#   User ubuntu
#   IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
#   ServerAliveInterval 60
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config

# 输出公钥，复制到 GitHub → Settings → SSH Keys
echo ""
echo "=== 你的 SSH 公钥（复制到 GitHub）==="
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

验证（添加 SSH Key 到 GitHub 后）： 

ssh -T git@github.com    # 应返回 "Hi username! You've successfully authenticated"

docs.title: Phase 4：VSCode
docs.text:
为什么用官方 .deb 而不是 Snap： Snap 版 VSCode 运行在沙箱中，对 /home 之外的路径、系统 socket、Go/Python 工具链的访问有限制，会导致 LSP 路径解析失败。官方 .deb 无此限制。

curl -fsSL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc \
  | gpg --dearmor \
  | sudo tee /usr/share/keyrings/packages.microsoft.gpg > /dev/null

echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/packages.microsoft.gpg] \
  https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/code stable main" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vscode.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y code
code --version

VSCode 核心配置（Ctrl+Shift+P → "Open User Settings JSON"）：

{
  "editor.fontFamily": "'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font', 'Courier New', monospace",
  "editor.fontSize": 14,
  "editor.lineHeight": 1.6,
  "editor.tabSize": 2,
  "editor.formatOnSave": true,
  "editor.minimap.enabled": false,
  "editor.bracketPairColorization.enabled": true,
  "editor.inlayHints.enabled": "on",
  "terminal.integrated.fontFamily": "'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font'",
  "terminal.integrated.fontSize": 13,
  "files.autoSave": "onFocusChange",
  "workbench.colorTheme": "Default Dark Modern",
  "explorer.confirmDelete": false,
  "[python]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "ms-python.black-formatter" },
  "[go]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "golang.go" },
  "[vue]": { "editor.defaultFormatter": "Vue.volar" }
}

扩展安装：

# 语言核心（必装）
code --install-extension golang.go
code --install-extension ms-python.python
code --install-extension ms-python.pylance
code --install-extension ms-python.black-formatter
code --install-extension Vue.volar

# 代码质量
code --install-extension esbenp.prettier-vscode
code --install-extension dbaeumer.vscode-eslint
code --install-extension streetsidesoftware.code-spell-checker

# Git
code --install-extension eamodio.gitlens
code --install-extension mhutchie.git-graph

# 容器与工具
code --install-extension ms-azuretools.vscode-docker
code --install-extension humao.rest-client
code --install-extension mechatroner.rainbow-csv
code --install-extension redhat.vscode-yaml

docs.title: Phase 5：Go 开发环境
docs.text:
为什么用官方 tarball： Ubuntu apt 仓库的 Go 版本通常落后官方 1–2 个版本。Go 的更新频率约每 6 个月一个大版本，工具链更新也依赖具体版本，建议直接用官方包。

# 获取最新版本号并安装
GO_LATEST=$(curl -s https://go.dev/VERSION?m=text | head -1)
echo "Installing: $GO_LATEST"

wget "https://go.dev/dl/${GO_LATEST}.linux-amd64.tar.gz" -O /tmp/go.tar.gz
sudo rm -rf /usr/local/go
sudo tar -C /usr/local -xzf /tmp/go.tar.gz
rm /tmp/go.tar.gz

source ~/.bashrc

验证： 

go version
go env GOPATH

安装核心 Go 工具：

go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest           # Language Server（VSCode Go 必需）
go install github.com/go-delve/delve/cmd/dlv@latest   # 调试器
go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest    # import 自动管理
go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest  # Linter
go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest             # 热重载（开发期自动重启）

常见失败模式： - gopls: command not found：$GOPATH/bin 未在 PATH 中，检查 ~/.config/devenv/env.sh - go install 报网络超时：配置 GOPROXY go env -w GOPROXY=https://goproxy.cn,direct（国内环境）

docs.title: 6.1 安装 pyenv
docs.text:
为什么 pyenv 而不是 conda： conda 适合数据科学场景（大量二进制依赖），AI 应用开发场景（API 调用、服务开发）不需要 conda 的复杂性。pyenv 轻量、与 uv 配合更好。

sudo apt install -y \
  libssl-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libreadline-dev \
  libsqlite3-dev libncursesw5-dev xz-utils tk-dev \
  libxml2-dev libxmlsec1-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev

curl https://pyenv.run | bash

source ~/.bashrc

# 安装 Python 3.12（AI 生态支持最好的稳定版本）
pyenv install 3.12.0
pyenv global 3.12.0

python --version    # Python 3.12.0

docs.title: 6.2 安装 uv
docs.text:
为什么 uv 而不是 pip/poetry： uv 用 Rust 实现，依赖解析和安装速度比 pip 快 10–100 倍，兼容 pip 语法，内置 venv 管理，2024 年后已成为 Python 工具链新标准。

curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
source ~/.bashrc
uv --version

AI 项目典型依赖（在项目目录中按需安装）：

cd ~/projects/my-ai-app
uv init .
uv add anthropic                  # Claude SDK
uv add openai                     # OpenAI 兼容接口（Ollama 也用此）
uv add langchain langchain-anthropic langchain-community
uv add qdrant-client              # Qdrant 向量数据库
uv add sentence-transformers      # 本地 Embedding（CPU）
uv add fastapi "uvicorn[standard]"  # API 服务
uv add redis                      # Redis 客户端
uv add python-dotenv              # .env 文件加载
uv add pytest pytest-asyncio      # 测试

说明：sentence-transformers 在 CPU 环境下默认安装 CPU 版 PyTorch（约 500MB），首次 import 时自动下载 Embedding 模型（约 90MB）。

docs.title: Phase 7：Node.js / 前端环境
docs.text:
为什么 nvm 而不是 apt： apt 的 Node.js 通常是旧 LTS 版本。前端生态对 Node.js 版本敏感，nvm 支持多版本共存。

curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.40.1/install.sh | bash
source ~/.bashrc

nvm install --lts
nvm use --lts
nvm alias default lts/*

node --version && npm --version

安装 pnpm（替代 npm）：

为什么 pnpm： pnpm 通过硬链接共享依赖，磁盘占用比 npm/yarn 低 60%，安装速度更快，在 monorepo 场景下优势尤为明显。

npm install -g pnpm
pnpm --version

# Vue 项目脚手架
pnpm add -g @vue/cli
# 或使用 Vite（推荐）：pnpm create vite my-app --template vue-ts

docs.title: Phase 8：Docker 容器环境
docs.text:
# 清理旧版本
sudo apt remove -y docker docker-engine docker.io containerd runc 2>/dev/null || true

# 安装 Docker Engine
sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg \
  | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg
sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg

echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.gpg] \
  https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu \
  $(lsb_release -cs) stable" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y \
  docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io \
  docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin

sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
sudo systemctl enable --now docker

注销并重新登录后验证： 

docker run hello-world
docker compose version

常见失败模式： - Got permission denied：未重新登录，newgrp docker 可临时生效 - lsb_release -cs 返回未知代号：手动查看 Docker 支持列表，Ubuntu 26.04 代号为 oracular 或 noble，确认后替换

docs.title: Phase 9：本地服务编排（docker-compose）
docs.text:
核心原则： 所有本地依赖服务通过一个 docker-compose.yml 统一管理，不手动 docker run。

mkdir -p ~/dev-services/data/{qdrant,postgres,redis,langfuse}
cd ~/dev-services

创建 ~/dev-services/docker-compose.yml：

# ~/dev-services/docker-compose.yml
# 本地开发服务编排：向量库 + 关系型DB + 缓存 + LLM可观测性
# 使用：docker compose up -d / docker compose down

services:

  # ── 向量数据库 ──────────────────────────────────────────
  qdrant:
    image: qdrant/qdrant:latest
    container_name: dev-qdrant
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6333:6333"    # HTTP / REST API
      - "6334:6334"    # gRPC
    volumes:
      - ./data/qdrant:/qdrant/storage
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:6333/healthz"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3

  # ── PostgreSQL + pgvector ────────────────────────────────
  postgres:
    image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
    container_name: dev-postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: devuser
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <LOCAL_DEV_POSTGRES_PASSWORD>
      POSTGRES_DB: devdb
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ./data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U devuser -d devdb"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # ── Redis ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: dev-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - ./data/redis:/data
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3

  # ── Langfuse（LLM 调用可观测性）────────────────────────
  langfuse:
    image: langfuse/langfuse:latest
    container_name: dev-langfuse
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://devuser:<LOCAL_DEV_POSTGRES_PASSWORD>@postgres:5432/langfuse
      NEXTAUTH_SECRET: <LOCAL_DEV_NEXTAUTH_SECRET>
      NEXTAUTH_URL: http://localhost:3000
      SALT: <LOCAL_DEV_SALT>

操作命令：

cd ~/dev-services

# 启动所有服务
docker compose up -d

# 查看状态
docker compose ps

# 查看日志
docker compose logs -f qdrant

# 停止（保留数据）
docker compose stop

# 停止并删除容器（数据保留在 ./data/）
docker compose down

验证所有服务：

# Qdrant
curl http://localhost:6333/healthz

# PostgreSQL
docker exec dev-postgres pg_isready -U devuser

# Redis
docker exec dev-redis redis-cli ping    # 应返回 PONG

# Langfuse Web UI：浏览器打开 http://localhost:3000
# 首次进入注册账号（本地账号，无需真实邮箱）

docs.title: 情况说明
docs.text:
Anthropic 官方不提供 Linux 版 Claude Desktop。官方为 Linux 开发者提供的路径是 Claude Code CLI（功能完整，支持 MCP）。

社区项目 aaddrick/claude-desktop-debian 通过重新打包官方 Windows Electron 包提供 Linux .deb 构建，是目前最成熟的非官方方案。两个选项适用于不同工作风格，按需选择，不需要都安装。

docs.title: 10.1 选项 A：Claude Code CLI（官方支持，推荐）
docs.text:
适合：终端工作流、AI 辅助编码、MCP 工具集成。

前置条件： Phase 7 已安装 Node.js。

npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
claude --version

# 认证（通过 API Key 或浏览器登录）
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="<ANTHROPIC_API_KEY>"
claude --print "Hello"    # 验证连通性

MCP 配置（连接本地工具）：

cat > ~/.claude.json << 'EOF'
{
  "mcpServers": {}
}
EOF

docs.title: 10.2 选项 B：非官方 Claude Desktop（社区维护）
docs.text:
适合：需要 GUI 界面、对话式工作流、不习惯纯终端。

⚠️ 声明： aaddrick/claude-desktop-debian 是社区项目，非 Anthropic 官方支持。 代码开源（MIT/Apache 双协议），使用 GPG 签名包，风险可控，但不等于官方安全背书。 安装前建议查阅项目 GitHub 页面确认最新状态。

# 安装前置依赖
sudo apt install -y libfuse2

# 添加 GPG Key 和仓库
curl -fsSL https://pkg.claude-desktop-debian.dev/KEY.gpg \
  | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/claude-desktop.gpg

echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/claude-desktop.gpg arch=amd64,arm64] \
  https://pkg.claude-desktop-debian.dev stable main" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/claude-desktop.list

sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y claude-desktop

GNOME Wayland 热键修复（如使用 Wayland 会话）： 

claude-desktop --install-gnome-hotkey

MCP 配置文件： ~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

验证： 从应用菜单启动，登录 Anthropic 账号。

docs.title: Phase 11：Ollama（本地 LLM）
docs.text:
为什么： 开发期间反复调用 Claude API 有成本。Ollama 在本地运行开源小模型，提供 OpenAI 兼容接口。用本地模型验证业务流程，用 Claude API 做集成测试和生产调用。

CPU 环境的现实预期： 7B 模型在 8 核 CPU 上推理速度约 2–8 token/s（明显慢于 GPU），适合用于流程验证，不适合交互式使用。

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

# 验证安装
ollama --version

# 拉取 CPU 友好的小模型
# qwen2.5:1.5b - 约 1GB，CPU 速度较快，适合快速验证
ollama pull qwen2.5:1.5b

# 测试
ollama run qwen2.5:1.5b "Hello, respond with one sentence"

Ollama 服务管理： 

# Ollama 安装后自动注册 systemd 服务
sudo systemctl status ollama
sudo systemctl enable ollama    # 开机自启

# Ollama 暴露 OpenAI 兼容接口
curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags    # 列出已下载的模型

在代码中切换本地/云端模型（Python 示例）：

import os
from openai import OpenAI

# 开发期：本地 Ollama
if os.getenv("ENV") == "development":
    client = OpenAI(
        base_url="http://localhost:11434/v1",
        api_key="ollama"    # Ollama 不验证 key，随便填
    )
    model = "qwen2.5:1.5b"
else:
    # 生产：真实 API
    import anthropic
    # 使用 anthropic 客户端

docs.title: Phase 12：项目工作流模板
docs.text: 这部分不是安装步骤，而是每个新项目创建时的标准初始化模板。

docs.title: 12.1 Makefile 模板（AI 后端项目）
docs.text:
# Makefile - AI 后端项目工作流标准化
# 用法：make dev / make test / make lint / make clean

.PHONY: dev test lint clean build docker-up docker-down

# ── 开发 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dev: docker-up
    uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

dev-go:
    air    # Go 热重载（需安装 github.com/air-verse/air）

# ── 测试 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
test:
    uv run pytest tests/ -v --tb=short

test-unit:
    uv run pytest tests/unit/ -v

test-integration:
    uv run pytest tests/integration/ -v -m integration

# ── 代码质量 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
lint:
    uv run ruff check app/ tests/
    uv run mypy app/

lint-go:
    golangci-lint run ./...

format:
    uv run ruff format app/ tests/

# ── Docker 服务 ──────────────────────────────────────────────
docker-up:
    cd ~/dev-services && docker compose up -d
    @echo "等待服务就绪..."
    @sleep 3
    @docker exec dev-redis redis-cli ping > /dev/null && echo "✓ Redis"
    @curl -sf http://localhost:6333/healthz > /dev/null && echo "✓ Qdrant"

docker-down:
    cd ~/dev-services && docker compose stop

# ── 构建 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
build:
    uv build

# ── 清理 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
clean:
    find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
    find . -name "*.pyc" -delete 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -rf .pytest_cache dist build

docs.title: 12.2 新项目初始化脚本
docs.text:
# ~/bin/new-ai-project（可执行，用于快速创建新项目）
cat > ~/.local/bin/new-ai-project << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -e

PROJECT_NAME="${1:?用法: new-ai-project <project-name>}"
PROJECT_DIR="$HOME/projects/$PROJECT_NAME"

mkdir -p "$PROJECT_DIR"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR"

# Git 初始化
git init
cp ~/.gitignore_global .gitignore

# direnv 配置
cat > .envrc << 'EOF'
# 项目环境变量 - 按需修改
export MODEL="claude-sonnet-4-6"
export LOG_LEVEL="debug"
EOF
direnv allow .

# Python 项目初始化
uv init .
uv add anthropic langchain langchain-anthropic qdrant-client fastapi "uvicorn[standard]" python-dotenv

# 复制 Makefile 模板
cp ~/templates/Makefile .

# 基础目录结构
mkdir -p app tests/{unit,integration} docs

echo "✓ 项目 $PROJECT_NAME 初始化完成: $PROJECT_DIR"
SCRIPT

chmod +x ~/.local/bin/new-ai-project
mkdir -p ~/projects ~/templates

docs.title: Phase 13：Langfuse 配置（LLM 可观测性）
docs.text:
为什么必须有可观测性： AI 应用的调试和优化依赖对每次 LLM 调用的追踪：prompt 内容、响应时间、token 消耗、检索命中率、向量相似度分布。没有这一层，debug 靠 print，优化靠猜。

Langfuse 已在 Phase 9 的 docker-compose 中包含，启动后访问 http://localhost:3000 注册本地账号。

Python 接入：

uv add langfuse

# 在项目入口处初始化
from langfuse import Langfuse
from langfuse.callback import CallbackHandler
import os

langfuse = Langfuse(
    public_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"],
    host="http://localhost:3000"
)

# 与 LangChain 集成
langfuse_handler = CallbackHandler(
    public_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"],
    secret_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"],
    host="http://localhost:3000"
)

# 使用
chain.invoke({"input": "..."}, config={"callbacks": [langfuse_handler]})

接入后可追踪： - 每次 LLM 调用的完整 prompt 和 response - Token 消耗（输入/输出分别统计） - 端到端 RAG pipeline 延迟分解 - 向量检索命中的 chunk 内容

docs.title: Phase 14：最终验证
docs.text:
echo "=============================="
echo "  AI 全栈环境最终验证"
echo "=============================="

# 系统
echo ""
echo "[系统]"
lsb_release -d | awk '{print "OS:", $2, $3}'
free -h | grep Mem | awk '{print "RAM:", $2}'
swapon --show | grep -v NAME | awk '{print "Swap:", $3}' || echo "Swap: 未配置"
systemctl is-enabled sleep.target 2>/dev/null | grep -q masked && echo "休眠: 已禁用" || echo "休眠: 未禁用"

# 工具链
echo ""
echo "[工具链]"
git --version
go version
python --version
node --version | awk '{print "Node.js:", $1}'
pnpm --version | awk '{print "pnpm:", $1}'
docker --version | awk '{print $1, $2, $3}'
docker compose version
uv --version
direnv --version | awk '{print "direnv:", $2}'
tmux -V
code --version | head -1 | awk '{print "VSCode:", $1}'

# AI 工具
echo ""
echo "[AI 工具]"
claude --version 2>/dev/null || echo "Claude Code CLI: 未安装"
ollama --version 2>/dev/null || echo "Ollama: 未安装"

# 本地服务
echo ""
echo "[本地服务]"
curl -sf http://localhost:6333/healthz > /dev/null \
  && echo "Qdrant: ✓ Running" || echo "Qdrant: ✗ Not running"
docker exec dev-redis redis-cli ping 2>/dev/null | grep -q PONG \
  && echo "Redis: ✓ Running" || echo "Redis: ✗ Not running"
docker exec dev-postgres pg_isready -U devuser -q 2>/dev/null \
  && echo "PostgreSQL: ✓ Running" || echo "PostgreSQL: ✗ Not running"
curl -sf http://localhost:3000 > /dev/null \
  && echo "Langfuse: ✓ Running (http://localhost:3000)" || echo "Langfuse: ✗ Not running"

# API 连通性
echo ""
echo "[API 连通性]"
[ -n "$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" ] && echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ✓ 已设置" || echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ✗ 未设置"

echo ""
echo "=============================="

端对端 API 测试：

# 创建临时测试目录和 venv
cd /tmp && mkdir -p api-test && cd api-test
uv init . --quiet
uv add anthropic --quiet

uv run python - << 'EOF'
import anthropic
import sys

try:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    msg = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=32,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Reply with exactly: API_OK"}]
    )
    print(f"Claude API: ✓ {msg.content[0].text}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Claude API: ✗ {e}", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)
EOF

cd ~ && rm -rf /tmp/api-test

docs.title: 日常更新
docs.text:
# 系统包（建议每周）
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

# Go 工具链（按需，追 gopls 更新）
go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest

# uv 自身（月度）
uv self update

# Node.js（LTS 大版本约每年一次）
nvm install --lts && nvm alias default lts/*

# Docker 镜像（按需）
cd ~/dev-services && docker compose pull && docker compose up -d

# Claude Code CLI（随 claude.ai 版本更新）
npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# Claude Desktop 非官方包（apt 源自动更新）
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y claude-desktop

# Ollama 模型
ollama pull qwen2.5:1.5b    # 更新到最新版本

docs.title: 关键文件备份清单
docs.text: 文件 / 目录 内容 是否入 Git ~/.config/devenv/env.sh 环境变量配置 ✓（无 secrets） ~/.config/dev-secrets/keys.env API Keys ✗ 绝不 ~/.ssh/config SSH 配置 ✓（脱敏后） ~/.gitignore_global 全局 gitignore ✓ ~/.tmux.conf tmux 配置 ✓ ~/dev-services/docker-compose.yml 本地服务编排 ✓ ~/dev-services/data/ 数据库数据 ✗（定期备份到其他位置）

docs.title: 手册覆盖边界
docs.text: 本手册不覆盖： - 网络代理 / 镜像源配置（依赖用户网络环境） - NVIDIA GPU / CUDA 安装 - 生产环境部署（Kubernetes、云服务器） - 团队共享开发环境（secrets 管理需重新设计）

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard summarizes safe operating practices for Kubernetes API gateways such as APISIX, Kong, or Nginx-based ingress gateways.

docs.title: Gateway Responsibilities
docs.text:
An API gateway commonly handles:

  • Routing.
  • TLS termination.
  • Authentication and authorization integration.
  • Rate limiting.
  • Request and response transformation.
  • Observability.
  • Canary or weighted routing.

docs.title: Deployment Checklist
docs.text:
Before production use:

  • Gateway controller is highly available.
  • Data plane pods have readiness and liveness probes.
  • Admin API is not publicly exposed.
  • Dashboard access is protected.
  • TLS certificate lifecycle is documented.
  • Metrics are scraped by Prometheus.
  • Access logs are routed to the logging platform.
  • Rate limiting and timeout defaults are defined.

docs.title: Route Template
docs.text:
Use placeholders in public examples:

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-gateway-route
  namespace: example-namespace
spec:
  ingressClassName: example-gateway
  rules:
    - host: api.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /v1
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: example-api
                port:
                  number: 80

docs.title: Security Controls
docs.text:
• Disable anonymous admin access.
  • Restrict admin endpoints by network policy.
  • Store admin keys in a secret manager.
  • Use separate routes for internal and external APIs.
  • Enforce request size and timeout limits.
  • Enable audit logging for route changes.

docs.title: Observability
docs.text:
Minimum metrics:

  • Request rate.
  • Error rate.
  • Latency percentiles.
  • Upstream health.
  • Gateway pod CPU and memory.
  • Route-level 4xx and 5xx counts.

docs.title: Change Procedure
docs.text:
1. Review route manifest.
  2. Validate service and port.
  3. Apply to staging.
  4. Run smoke test.
  5. Apply to production.
  6. Confirm metrics and logs.
  7. Record rollback command.

Rollback:

kubectl -n example-namespace rollout undo deployment/example-api
kubectl -n example-namespace delete ingress example-gateway-route

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Kubernetes Storage and Ingress Standard
  • Prometheus Alerting Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes public-safe operating guidance for DolphinScheduler or similar workflow schedulers.

docs.title: Operational Areas
docs.text:
• Metadata database backup.
  • Worker capacity.
  • Task retry policy.
  • Alerting.
  • Permission model.
  • Dependency isolation.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
• Scheduler process healthy.
  • Worker process healthy.
  • Metadata database reachable.
  • Test workflow can run.
  • Alerts are delivered.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Prometheus Alerting Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes public-safe deployment, migration, and validation practices for Kafka and ZooKeeper.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Use this for legacy ZooKeeper-backed Kafka clusters and migration planning. For new systems, evaluate modern Kafka modes and managed streaming services.

docs.title: Planning Checklist
docs.text: Area Questions Brokers How many brokers and what storage class? ZooKeeper Is ZooKeeper still required by the Kafka version? Topics What partitions, replication factors, and retention policies are required? Clients Can producers and consumers tolerate rolling restarts? Security Are authentication, authorization, and TLS required? Observability Are broker, topic, consumer lag, and JVM metrics collected?

docs.title: Deployment Principles
docs.text:
1. Keep broker IDs stable.
  2. Store data on dedicated persistent volumes.
  3. Use replication factor greater than one for critical topics.
  4. Monitor under-replicated partitions and consumer lag.
  5. Test producer and consumer behavior before production cutover.

docs.title: Migration Flow
docs.text:
1. Freeze topic topology changes.
  2. Capture topic list, partition count, replication factor, and consumer groups.
  3. Deploy target cluster.
  4. Configure secure connectivity between source and target.
  5. Mirror or copy data using an approved migration tool.
  6. Validate topic offsets and consumer lag.
  7. Cut over producers.
  8. Cut over consumers.
  9. Keep source cluster read-only until rollback window closes.

docs.title: Validation Commands
docs.text:
kafka-topics.sh --bootstrap-server broker.example.com:9092 --list
kafka-consumer-groups.sh --bootstrap-server broker.example.com:9092 --all-groups --describe
kafka-topics.sh --bootstrap-server broker.example.com:9092 --describe --topic example-topic

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text: Rollback depends on whether producers have written to the target. Define the cutoff point before migration. If bidirectional writes are not supported, rollback requires stopping target writes and restoring producer routing to the source.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Prometheus Exporter Monitoring Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes how to operate Nacos as a configuration and service discovery component.

docs.title: Deployment Principles
docs.text:
• Use a cluster for production.
  • Store metadata in a managed or highly available database.
  • Restrict console access.
  • Back up configuration data.
  • Monitor JVM, database connectivity, and service registration health.

docs.title: Kubernetes Checklist
docs.text:
kubectl get statefulset -n example-namespace
kubectl get svc -n example-namespace
kubectl logs -n example-namespace statefulset/example-nacos

docs.title: Risk Areas
docs.text:
• Configuration changes can affect many services.
  • Database credentials must be secret-managed.
  • Console access should not be public.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Kubernetes Cluster Provisioning Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines public-safe HAProxy reverse proxy and load balancing practices.

docs.title: Basic Pattern
docs.text:
frontend example_http
  bind *:80
  default_backend example_backend

backend example_backend
  balance roundrobin
  option httpchk GET /health
  server app1 10.0.0.10:8080 check
  server app2 10.0.0.11:8080 check

docs.title: Operational Checks
docs.text:
haproxy -c -f /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg
systemctl reload haproxy
systemctl status haproxy --no-pager

docs.title: Safety Notes
docs.text:
• Do not publish real backend addresses.
  • Use health checks that match application readiness.
  • Use TLS termination only with managed certificate lifecycle.
  • Restrict stats page access.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text: • Nginx Reverse Proxy and TLS Standard

docs.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy and TLS Standard
Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines safe public patterns for deploying Nginx or OpenResty as a reverse proxy with TLS.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
1. Separate public listener configuration from upstream service configuration.
  2. Use TLS certificates from a controlled lifecycle process.
  3. Do not publish real certificates, private keys, domains, or upstream addresses.
  4. Use health checks and reload validation before applying changes.
  5. Keep access logs useful while avoiding sensitive request data where possible.

docs.title: Basic Reverse Proxy Template
docs.text:
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name service.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://example-upstream;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_connect_timeout 5s;
        proxy_send_timeout 60s;
        proxy_read_timeout 60s;
    }
}

Upstream example:

upstream example-upstream {
    server 10.0.0.10:8080 max_fails=3 fail_timeout=30s;
    server 10.0.0.11:8080 max_fails=3 fail_timeout=30s;
}

docs.title: TLS Template
docs.text:
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name service.example.com;

    ssl_certificate /etc/nginx/certs/service.example.com.crt;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/nginx/certs/service.example.com.key;
    ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
    ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://example-upstream;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;
    }
}

The key path is illustrative. Never commit actual private key files.

docs.title: Certificate Expiry Check
docs.text:
echo | openssl s_client -servername service.example.com -connect service.example.com:443 2>/dev/null \
  | openssl x509 -noout -dates

For local files:

openssl x509 -in /etc/nginx/certs/service.example.com.crt -noout -dates

docs.title: Reload Procedure
docs.text:
sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx
sudo systemctl status nginx --no-pager

Validation:

curl -I https://service.example.com
curl -sS https://service.example.com/health

docs.title: Common Failure Modes
docs.text: Symptom Likely Cause Check 502 Bad Gateway Upstream unavailable or wrong port curl upstream from proxy host TLS warning Expired or wrong certificate openssl x509 -noout -dates Redirect loop Incorrect forwarded protocol Check X-Forwarded-Proto handling Large upload fails Body size too small client_max_body_size Long request times out Proxy timeout too low proxy_read_timeout

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text:
• Store private keys with restrictive permissions.
  • Use automation for renewal where possible.
  • Test renewal in staging before production.
  • Avoid logging authorization headers.
  • Do not expose admin routes through public listeners unless explicitly required and protected.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines safe operational practices for OpenVPN-based administrative access.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Use VPN for administrative access, not as a substitute for least privilege.
  • Issue named user certificates.
  • Revoke unused certificates.
  • Restrict routes to required networks.
  • Log connections without exposing private keys.

docs.title: Operational Checklist
docs.text:
• Server certificate is valid.
  • Client certificates are tracked.
  • Revocation list is active.
  • Firewall allows only required VPN traffic.
  • Admin access is logged.

docs.title: Security Notes
docs.text: Never publish VPN profiles, private keys, user certificates, or shared secrets.

  • Edge device customization.

docs.title: Safety Rules
docs.text:
• Do not publish real Wi-Fi passwords.
  • Do not publish VPN profiles or keys.
  • Do not publish ISP account details.
  • Keep firmware build notes separate from live configuration.

docs.title: Validation

uci show network
uci show firewall
logread | tail

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes public-safe Samba file sharing operations.

docs.title: Controls
docs.text:
• Use named users and groups.
  • Restrict shares by path and permission.
  • Avoid guest write access.
  • Back up shared data.
  • Monitor disk usage.
  • Keep audit logs where required.

docs.title: Example Share
docs.text:
[example-share]
  path = /srv/samba/example
  browsable = yes
  read only = no
  valid users = @example-team

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
testparm
smbclient -L //files.example.internal -U example-user

docs.title: Publication Safety
docs.text: Do not publish real share names, user lists, internal hosts, or access paths.

docs.title: DevOps 深度文章质量标准
docs.text: • Standards description: Quality standard for DevOps deep articles: context, constraints, evidence, conclusions.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This standard defines how to decide whether a source file, note, diagram, runbook, script, or case from the Trash review workspace is valuable enough to become a public DevOps knowledge base article.

It is used for three goals:

  • Select high-value source material for the public DevOps knowledge base.
  • Rewrite internal or source-specific material into safe, reusable engineering guidance.
  • Prepare retrieval-friendly documents for a future DevOps Q&A assistant.

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text:
A deep DevOps article should not only explain what to run. It should explain the operational problem, the constraints, the design choice, the trade-offs, the implementation path, the validation method, and the rollback strategy.

If a document only contains commands, raw configuration, screenshots, credentials, logs, or environment-specific notes, it is not a deep article by itself. It may still be useful as source material, but it must be rewritten, merged, or excluded.

docs.title: Publication Gate
docs.text:
No source material may become public content unless it passes all publication gates.

Gate Requirement Fail Result Public safety No secrets, keys, tokens, passwords, private certificates, internal domains, private IPs, customer names, personal data, private repositories, raw SQL dumps, production configs, or unreleased business details. Exclude or quarantine. Anonymization Project, customer, team, user, region, asset, domain, repository, account, and business names are replaced with generic placeholders. Rewrite before publication. Reusable value The material teaches a repeatable DevOps pattern, method, checklist, standard, or decision model. Merge into FAQ or exclude. Engineering context The article explains scenario, constraints, assumptions, and target state. Downgrade unless context can be reconstructed safely. Operational closure The article includes validation, rollback, risk, and maintenance guidance when operational change is involved. Rewrite before publication. Retrieval quality Headings are stable, sections are small, and each section answers one operational question. Restructure before publication.

docs.title: 1. Problem-First
docs.text:
The article begins with the operational problem, not the tool.

Good:

  • A cluster upgrade needs to reduce workload disruption.
  • A CI/CD process needs safer rollback and auditability.
  • A logging platform needs predictable retention and query performance.
  • A cloud network migration needs isolation, route control, and validation.

Weak:

  • How to install a tool without explaining why it matters.
  • A list of commands copied from a shell history.
  • A diagram with no explanation of constraints or target state.

docs.title: 2. Clear Scope And Boundaries
docs.text:
The article states when the guidance applies and when it does not.

Examples:

  • Applies to production Kubernetes clusters with multiple node groups.
  • Applies to single-host Docker workloads, not production orchestration.
  • Applies to log aggregation patterns, not application-level logging design.
  • Applies to cloud migration planning, not raw customer network replication.

docs.title: 3. Architecture Or Process Judgment
docs.text:
The article explains why a method is selected.

Examples:

  • Why use GitOps instead of ad hoc deployment scripts.
  • Why separate build, release, and deploy permissions.
  • Why prefer managed add-ons for EKS control plane integrations.
  • Why keep stateful data outside disposable compute nodes.

docs.title: 4. Trade-Offs
docs.text:
The article names the cost of the recommendation.

Common DevOps trade-offs:

  • Availability vs cost.
  • Standardization vs flexibility.
  • Automation vs manual control.
  • Managed services vs self-managed control.
  • Security isolation vs operational complexity.
  • Fast delivery vs release governance.

docs.title: 5. Implementation Path
docs.text:
The article provides a safe sequence:

  1. Preparation.
  2. Baseline capture.
  3. Change plan.
  4. Execution.
  5. Validation.
  6. Rollback.
  7. Handoff and maintenance.

docs.title: 6. Validation And Rollback
docs.text:
The article explains how to prove success and how to recover.

Validation should include:

  • What command, metric, log, dashboard, or user journey to check.
  • Expected healthy output or behavior.
  • Failure signals.
  • Post-change observation window.

Rollback should include:

  • What can be reverted.
  • What cannot be safely reverted.
  • What data or state must be preserved.
  • Who approves rollback in a production-like environment.

docs.title: 7. Security Boundary
docs.text:
The article treats security as part of the engineering design.

Security sections should cover relevant points:

  • Identity and access control.
  • Secret storage and rotation.
  • Network exposure and ingress.
  • CI/CD supply-chain risk.
  • Log and artifact leakage.
  • Backup and restore access.
  • Least privilege for automation.

docs.title: 8. Reusable Model
docs.text:
The article should produce a reusable model, not just a one-off story.

Examples of reusable models:

  • Kubernetes upgrade readiness model.
  • Deployment rollback decision model.
  • Observability onboarding model.
  • Cloud network migration validation model.
  • Secrets handling model for CI/CD.
  • Public documentation sanitization model.

docs.title: Standards
docs.text:
Use when the content defines a repeatable operating baseline.

Required sections:

  • Purpose.
  • When To Use.
  • Prerequisites.
  • Standard Procedure.
  • Validation.
  • Rollback or Recovery.
  • Security Notes.
  • FAQ.
  • Related Documents.

Examples:

  • Docker Host Operations Standard.
  • Kubernetes Cluster Provisioning Standard.
  • Terraform IaC Standard.

docs.title: Runbooks
docs.text:
Use when the content guides incident response or recurring operational action.

Required sections:

  • Symptom.
  • Impact.
  • Triage.
  • Decision Tree.
  • Remediation.
  • Validation.
  • Escalation.
  • Rollback.
  • Prevention.

Examples:

  • PVC Usage Alert Runbook.
  • TLS Certificate Expiry Runbook.
  • Deployment Rollback Runbook.

docs.title: Migration Cases
docs.text:
Use when the content comes from a project or architecture change and can be anonymized.

Required sections:

  • Anonymous Scenario.
  • Source And Target State.
  • Constraints.
  • Migration Strategy.
  • Phases.
  • Validation.
  • Rollback.
  • Risks.
  • Lessons Learned.
  • Reusable Checklist.

The case must be rewritten as a general standard approach. Do not preserve customer-specific topology, business workflow, domains, internal service names, cost tables, or private architecture details.

docs.title: Troubleshooting Articles
docs.text:
Use when the source explains a failure mode or recurring diagnostic pattern.

Required sections:

  • Symptom.
  • Scope.
  • Likely Causes.
  • Diagnostic Commands.
  • Evidence Interpretation.
  • Fix Options.
  • Validation.
  • Prevention.

docs.title: Automation Articles
docs.text:
Use when the source contains scripts or repeatable automation.

Required sections:

  • Problem Automated.
  • Inputs.
  • Outputs.
  • Required Permissions.
  • Failure Modes.
  • Dry Run Or Safe Mode.
  • Logging.
  • Rollback Or Manual Recovery.
  • Security Notes.

Never publish raw scripts that contain credentials, internal paths, production hostnames, account IDs, or private operational logic.

docs.title: Scoring Matrix
docs.text:
Score each candidate from 0 to 5.

Dimension 0 3 5 Problem value No clear problem. Useful but narrow operational problem. Common high-value DevOps problem with clear impact. Context completeness No scenario or assumptions. Some environment and constraints. Clear scope, constraints, target state, and non-goals. Engineering depth Commands only. Some reasoning and steps. Architecture/process judgment with trade-offs. Reusability One-off record. Can become a checklist or FAQ. Can become a standard, runbook, or model. Validation and rollback Missing. Basic validation only. Clear validation, failure signals, rollback, and maintenance. Security posture Risk unknown. Can be sanitized with effort. Cleanly anonymized with explicit safety guidance. Retrieval quality Hard to chunk or cite. Some useful headings. Stable headings, focused sections, direct Q&A value. Freshness Obsolete or version-locked. Needs version notes. Current or written as version-aware guidance.

docs.title: Rating Decision
docs.text:
Rating Rule Action A Score >= 32, security risk <= 2, and reusable value >= 4. Rewrite as a standalone public article. B Score 22-31, security risk <= 3, and at least one strong reusable section. Merge into an existing standard, runbook, FAQ, or checklist. C Score 12-21 or context is incomplete but some experience is useful. Keep as internal review note or extract a small sanitized snippet. D Score < 12, security risk >= 4, or mostly private/business/runtime material. Exclude, quarantine, archive, or delete according to cleanup policy.

Security risk is judged separately:

Security Risk Meaning 0 Public-safe generic material. 1 Minor identifiers that are easy to remove. 2 Some environment details requiring careful rewrite. 3 Heavy anonymization required; publish only if value is high. 4 Contains sensitive or private operational content; do not publish raw. 5 Contains credentials, keys, secrets, production data, private customer data, or personal data; quarantine or destroy.

docs.title: Source Review Output Format
docs.text:
When reviewing a file, use this format:

Conclusion: A/B/C/D
Recommended action: standalone article / merge / snippet / exclude / quarantine
Reason:
Security risk: 0-5
Scores:
- Problem value:
- Context completeness:
- Engineering depth:
- Reusability:
- Validation and rollback:
- Security posture:
- Retrieval quality:
- Freshness:
Potential public title:
Target directory:
Required anonymization:
Missing information:
Next step:

docs.title: Replace Specific Identifiers
docs.text:
Use placeholders:

<cluster-name>
<namespace>
<service-name>
<region>
<account-id>
<vpc-id>
<subnet-id>
<example.com>
<private-registry>
<repository-url>
<customer-name>
<team-name>

docs.title: Rewrite Raw Commands
docs.text:
Commands must be safe examples, not production history.

Bad:

kubectl config use-context real-prod-cluster
kubectl delete pod real-customer-payment-7d9d

Good:

kubectl config use-context <cluster-name>
kubectl -n <namespace> rollout status deployment/<deployment-name>

docs.title: Rewrite Architecture Cases
docs.text:
Do not publish original architecture diagrams, customer routes, service names, or network topology. Convert them into a generalized pattern:

  • Source state.
  • Target state.
  • Migration constraints.
  • Reusable design decision.
  • Validation checklist.

docs.title: Retrieval-Friendly Writing Rules
docs.text:
For Q&A assistant readiness:

  • Use descriptive headings.
  • Keep each section focused on one question or procedure.
  • Prefer short paragraphs and explicit lists.
  • Include definitions for ambiguous operational terms.
  • Avoid hidden assumptions.
  • Add validation and rollback sections even when short.
  • Link related documents using stable relative links.

Each article should answer likely questions such as:

  • When should I use this?
  • What are the prerequisites?
  • What is the safest implementation order?
  • How do I validate success?
  • How do I roll back?
  • What are the security risks?
  • What should I monitor after the change?

docs.title: Minimum Publication Checklist
docs.text:
Before a document is published:

  • It has a clear title and stable path.
  • It is linked from mkdocs.yml.
  • It contains no raw secrets, production data, or private identifiers.
  • It has public-safe placeholders.
  • It includes validation and rollback where relevant.
  • It includes security notes where relevant.
  • It builds successfully with MkDocs.
  • It is suitable for both human reading and retrieval-based Q&A.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes a safe process for upgrading a managed or self-managed Kubernetes cluster. It is written for public reuse and does not depend on any private environment.

docs.title: When To Use
docs.text:
Use this procedure when:

  • Upgrading Amazon EKS, self-managed Kubernetes, or a Kubernetes-compatible platform.
  • Moving workloads from an older minor version to a newer supported minor version.
  • Replacing worker node groups during a platform upgrade.
  • Upgrading core add-ons such as CNI, CoreDNS, kube-proxy, ingress controllers, CSI drivers, monitoring, or logging agents.

docs.title: Upgrade Principles
docs.text:
1. Upgrade one minor version at a time unless the platform explicitly supports a larger jump.
  2. Treat add-ons as part of the cluster, not as optional extras.
  3. Upgrade control plane, add-ons, and nodes in a controlled sequence.
  4. Use new node groups for workload migration instead of in-place mutation when possible.
  5. Validate both Kubernetes health and application behavior before declaring the upgrade complete.
  6. Keep rollback paths available until post-upgrade monitoring is stable.

docs.title: Pre-Upgrade Assessment
docs.text:
Collect this baseline before making changes:

kubectl version --short
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get deployment,statefulset,daemonset -A
kubectl get ingress -A
kubectl get storageclass
kubectl get pvc -A
kubectl top nodes
kubectl top pods -A

Review these compatibility areas:

Area Check Kubernetes APIs Deprecated APIs removed in the target version. Add-ons CNI, CoreDNS, kube-proxy, CSI drivers, ingress controller, cert-manager, metrics-server. Workloads Pod disruption budgets, probes, resource requests, priority classes, security contexts. Storage CSI driver compatibility, PVC binding behavior, snapshot and backup support. Networking Ingress class behavior, load balancer annotations, service type changes, network policies. Observability Prometheus scrape targets, alert rules, dashboards, log collectors. Access IAM roles, RBAC, service accounts, cluster admin access, break-glass access.

docs.title: 1. Freeze Risky Changes
docs.text: Pause unrelated application deployments and infrastructure changes during the upgrade window. Communicate the maintenance window, expected impact, rollback criteria, and owner contacts.

docs.title: 2. Back Up Critical State
docs.text:
For managed clusters, export manifests and configuration:

kubectl get all -A -o yaml > cluster-workloads.snapshot.yaml
kubectl get configmap,ingress,svc,pvc,storageclass -A -o yaml > cluster-config.snapshot.yaml

Do not export raw Kubernetes Secret values into plain text files. Back up secrets through the platform-approved encrypted backup process or secret management system.

For self-managed clusters, back up etcd using the platform-recommended encrypted method.

docs.title: 3. Upgrade Control Plane
docs.text:
Upgrade the control plane through the cloud provider or cluster management tool. Wait until the API server is healthy and all core control plane checks pass.

Validation:

kubectl get --raw='/readyz?verbose'
kubectl get componentstatuses
kubectl get nodes

docs.title: 4. Upgrade Core Add-ons
docs.text:
Upgrade add-ons to versions compatible with the target Kubernetes version:

  • CNI plugin.
  • CoreDNS.
  • kube-proxy.
  • CSI drivers.
  • Metrics server.
  • Ingress controller.
  • Observability agents.

Validation:

kubectl get pods -n kube-system
kubectl rollout status deployment/coredns -n kube-system
kubectl get daemonset -n kube-system

docs.title: 5. Create New Worker Node Group
docs.text:
Create a new node group with the target Kubernetes-compatible image, runtime, IAM role, labels, taints, and scaling configuration.

Validation:

kubectl get nodes -L kubernetes.io/os,kubernetes.io/arch
kubectl describe node <new-node-name>

docs.title: 6. Drain Old Nodes Gradually
docs.text:
Drain old nodes in small batches:

kubectl cordon <old-node-name>
kubectl drain <old-node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data --timeout=10m

After each batch, validate application health before continuing.

docs.title: 7. Validate Workloads
docs.text:
Check:

kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'
kubectl get ingress -A
kubectl get pvc -A

Application validation should include:

  • HTTP health checks.
  • Login or core API smoke tests.
  • Background job processing.
  • Database connection checks.
  • External callback or webhook checks.
  • Alerting and dashboard visibility.

docs.title: 8. Decommission Old Node Group
docs.text: After the observation window passes, scale down and remove the old node group. Keep snapshots and upgrade notes for review.

docs.title: Rollback Strategy
docs.text:
Managed Kubernetes control plane upgrades are often not directly reversible. Practical rollback usually means:

  • Stop node migration.
  • Keep or recreate old node groups if still supported.
  • Roll back add-ons to compatible versions when safe.
  • Roll back application deployments.
  • Restore from backups if stateful components are affected.

Define rollback triggers before starting:

  • Core business smoke test fails.
  • Error rate exceeds threshold for more than 10 minutes.
  • Critical pods cannot schedule.
  • Storage attach or mount errors affect stateful workloads.
  • Ingress or load balancer behavior breaks external traffic.

docs.title: Post-Upgrade Review
docs.text:
Record:

  • Source and target versions.
  • Add-on versions before and after.
  • Node group changes.
  • Incidents and mitigations.
  • Deprecated API findings.
  • Follow-up platform work.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard defines a repeatable process for creating Kubernetes clusters on managed platforms such as EKS and CCE, or self-managed platforms built with kubeadm.

docs.title: Provisioning Principles
docs.text:
1. Separate cluster planning from cluster creation.
  2. Use infrastructure as code or declarative configuration where possible.
  3. Keep network, IAM, storage, ingress, observability, and CI/CD requirements in the initial design.
  4. Install only required add-ons.
  5. Validate each platform layer before deploying applications.

docs.title: Planning Checklist
docs.text: Area Questions Version Which Kubernetes version is supported by the platform and add-ons? Network What VPC, subnets, service CIDR, pod CIDR, and ingress model are required? Nodes What instance types, architectures, labels, taints, and scaling rules are required? Storage Which CSI drivers and default StorageClasses are needed? Access Who has admin access, deploy access, and read-only access? Ingress Which ingress controller and load balancer mode will be used? Observability How will metrics, logs, dashboards, and alerts be deployed? CI/CD How will images be built, pushed, and deployed?

docs.title: Standard Add-on Order
docs.text:
Install and validate in this order:

  1. Core DNS and kube-proxy compatibility.
  2. CNI plugin.
  3. CSI drivers for block and shared storage.
  4. Metrics server.
  5. Ingress controller.
  6. Cluster autoscaler or managed node autoscaling.
  7. Observability stack.
  8. GitOps or CI/CD deploy tooling.
  9. Security policy and admission controls.

docs.title: Managed Cluster Creation Flow
docs.text:
Use the provider tool or infrastructure as code to create:

  • Cluster control plane.
  • Node groups.
  • IAM roles or workload identity.
  • Security groups or network policies.
  • Add-on versions.

Validation:

kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get storageclass
kubectl get ingressclass

docs.title: Self-Managed kubeadm Flow
docs.text:
Before kubeadm init:

  • Disable swap.
  • Load bridge network modules.
  • Configure container runtime.
  • Configure system time.
  • Confirm hostnames and DNS.
  • Plan control plane endpoint for HA.

Validation:

kubeadm version
kubectl get nodes
kubectl get pods -n kube-system

docs.title: Application Readiness Gate
docs.text:
Do not deploy business applications until these checks pass:

  • Nodes are Ready.
  • System pods are healthy.
  • Metrics server returns data.
  • Default StorageClass is correct.
  • Ingress test endpoint works.
  • Cluster autoscaling behavior is understood.
  • Backup and restore process is documented.
  • Alert routing is tested.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• EKS and Kubernetes Upgrade Standard
  • Kubernetes Storage and Ingress Standard
  • Prometheus Alerting Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This standard describes how to design and validate Kubernetes storage and ingress for public reusable guidance.

docs.title: Storage Classes
docs.text:
Use separate StorageClasses for different workload types:

Storage Type Typical Use Notes Block storage Databases and single-writer stateful workloads Usually ReadWriteOnce. Shared file storage Multi-pod shared files, uploads, reports Often ReadWriteMany. Ephemeral storage Cache or temporary processing Must tolerate data loss.

docs.title: CSI Driver Checklist
docs.text:
Before production use:

  • Driver version matches Kubernetes version.
  • IAM or workload identity is configured.
  • Node plugin pods are healthy.
  • Controller plugin pods are healthy.
  • Volume provisioning works.
  • Volume expansion works if required.
  • Snapshot support is tested if required.

Validation:

kubectl get csidriver
kubectl get pods -A | grep csi
kubectl get storageclass

docs.title: PVC Test
docs.text:
Create a test PVC and pod in a non-production namespace:

apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: example-pvc
  namespace: example-namespace
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
  storageClassName: example-storage-class

Validate:

kubectl get pvc -n example-namespace
kubectl describe pvc example-pvc -n example-namespace

docs.title: Ingress Controller Checklist
docs.text:
Before exposing services:

  • IngressClass exists.
  • Controller pods are healthy.
  • Load balancer annotations are documented.
  • TLS certificate lifecycle is defined.
  • Default backend behavior is understood.
  • Access logs and metrics are enabled.
  • Health checks match application behavior.

Validation:

kubectl get ingressclass
kubectl get ingress -A
kubectl get svc -A | grep LoadBalancer

docs.title: Ingress Template
docs.text:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: example-namespace
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: "20m"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - service.example.com
      secretName: example-tls
  rules:
    - host: service.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: example-service
                port:
                  number: 80

Do not commit real TLS secrets.

docs.title: Common Failure Modes
docs.text: Area Symptom Check Storage PVC stuck pending StorageClass, CSI controller, IAM, quota Storage Pod mount failure Node plugin, filesystem, access mode Ingress 404 Ingress class, path, service selector Ingress 502 Backend pod readiness or service port Ingress TLS error Secret, certificate chain, host mismatch

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• NFS PVC Capacity Management
  • Nginx Reverse Proxy and TLS Standard

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This document defines a standard approach for monitoring and managing Kubernetes PersistentVolumeClaim capacity when NFS or NFS-like shared storage is used.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text: Kubernetes can show requested PVC capacity, but it does not always provide actual filesystem usage for NFS-backed paths. If usage is not monitored at the storage path level, applications can fail unexpectedly when the underlying shared filesystem fills up.

docs.title: Standard Approach
docs.text:
Use a two-layer model:

  1. Kubernetes metadata: namespace, PVC name, PV, StorageClass, requested size.
  2. Storage filesystem usage: actual path usage from the NFS server or mounted host.

The monitoring system should join both views and alert when usage crosses defined thresholds.

docs.title: Recommended Thresholds
docs.text: Level Usage Response Warning 75% Review growth rate and owner. High 85% Plan cleanup, expansion, or migration. Critical 90% Take immediate action and notify service owner. Emergency 95% Stop nonessential writes and execute expansion or cleanup.

docs.title: Required Metadata
docs.text:
Track these fields for each PVC:

cluster
namespace
pvc_name
pv_name
storage_class
requested_capacity
storage_path
actual_used
actual_available
usage_percent
owner
service
last_checked_at

docs.title: 1. Identify NFS StorageClasses
docs.text:
kubectl get storageclass
kubectl describe storageclass <storage-class-name>

Record provisioner, server, path pattern, reclaim policy, and expansion support.

docs.title: 2. List PVCs Using the StorageClass
docs.text:
kubectl get pvc -A -o wide
kubectl get pv

Map each PVC to its PV and storage path.

docs.title: 3. Measure Filesystem Usage
docs.text:
On the storage server or mounted observation host:

df -h <storage-root>
du -sh <storage-root>/<namespace>/<pvc-name>

Use a consistent path convention. If dynamic provisioning creates hashed paths, maintain a PV-to-path mapping.

docs.title: 4. Alert on Usage
docs.text:
Alert messages should include:

Cluster: example-cluster
Namespace: example-namespace
PVC: example-pvc
StorageClass: example-nfs
Usage: 87%
Threshold: 85%
Recommended Action: review growth and expand or clean up

Do not include credentials, real internal domains, or sensitive mount paths in public alert examples.

docs.title: 5. Remediate
docs.text:
Common remediation options:

  • Remove expired application files.
  • Archive old logs or attachments.
  • Expand the backing NFS volume.
  • Migrate a high-growth workload to a dedicated volume.
  • Add retention policies at application or storage level.

docs.title: Cleanup Safety
docs.text:
Never delete PVC data using a generic script unless all of these are true:

  • The owner approved the cleanup.
  • The target namespace and PVC are explicitly selected.
  • A backup or snapshot exists if data is business-critical.
  • The script has a dry-run mode.
  • The cleanup rule uses age, path, and file type filters.
  • The action logs every deleted object.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
After remediation:

df -h <storage-root>
du -sh <storage-root>/<namespace>/<pvc-name>
kubectl get pvc -n <namespace>
kubectl describe pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace>

Verify the affected application can still read and write expected data.

docs.title: Why not rely only on kubectl get pvc?
docs.text: PVC capacity shows requested storage, not always real usage. NFS-backed storage requires filesystem-level observation.

docs.title: Should cleanup be automatic?
docs.text: Automatic cleanup is acceptable only for clearly disposable data such as temporary files. Business data should require owner approval or a policy-controlled retention system.

docs.title: DevOps 专家知识库
docs.text:
DevOps 不是“会写脚本的人”，也不是“会用 Kubernetes 的运维”。它是一套把软件交付、生产运行、可靠性、安全治理和组织反馈连接起来的工程能力系统。

这个站点不按工具分类，而按一个资深 DevOps 看待生产系统的方式组织：

系统如何设计、变更如何交付、失败如何恢复、问题如何被看见、能力如何平台化、经验如何复盘。

docs.title: 从能力地图开始
docs.text:
站点的“宪法层”是能力地图，它回答三个问题，再决定你接下来读什么：

  1. DevOps 能力拓扑 —— DevOps 由哪些层级能力组成？从基础层到组织反馈层的能力栈。
  2. 价值与成熟度 —— DevOps 价值如何定义？L1 工具化到 L5 自适应的五级模型。
  3. 自评与成长路径 —— 你是 Ops、初级 DevOps，还是接近架构师或专家？六维度自评矩阵。

不确定先读什么？看内容成熟度地图，它诚实标注了每个栏目的状态。

docs.text: 栏目 回答的生产问题 能力地图 我该如何理解 DevOps，而不是背工具清单？ 系统设计 设计能进生产的系统，边界和取舍是什么？ 变更交付 变更安全送到生产，怎么降低失败率？ 可靠运行 系统坏了能不能看见、定位、恢复、复盘？ 平台治理 多团队共用标准，同时控权限、安全、成本？ 案例复盘 真实项目和错误决策里有什么可复用判断？ 标准手册 查具体标准、步骤、模板、标签索引。

docs.title: 能力地图
docs.text:
DevOps 不是“会写脚本的人”，也不是“会用 Kubernetes 的运维”。它是一套把软件交付、生产运行、可靠性、安全治理和组织反馈连接起来的工程能力系统。

本站不是工具目录。它按一个资深 DevOps 看待生产系统的方式组织：系统如何设计、变更如何交付、失败如何恢复、问题如何被看见、能力如何平台化、经验如何复盘。

这一级是站点的“宪法层”，回答三个问题，再决定你接下来读什么：

页面 它回答的问题 DevOps 能力拓扑 DevOps 到底由哪些层级能力组成？ 价值与成熟度 DevOps 的价值如何定义，成熟度如何评级？ 自评与成长路径 我现在是 Ops、初级 DevOps，还是接近架构师或专家？ 内容成熟度地图 站点哪些栏目已成熟、哪些还在建设？我该先读什么？

读完这一级，你应该能判断这个站点是否对你有价值，以及最值得先读的是哪一类内容。

docs.title: 内容成熟度地图
docs.text: 这个站点正在建设中。与其用空栏目假装完整，不如直接告诉你每一块的真实状态，以及最值得先读的内容。

docs.title: 成熟度图例
docs.text:
• 成熟：已有可信、可直接参考的实体内容。
  • 参考手册：操作标准，适合查具体步骤，但不替代深度文章。
  • 待补旗舰：栏目框架已立，但深度旗舰文章尚未写出。
  • 建设中：仅有定位说明，内容待沉淀。

docs.title: 各栏目状态
docs.text: 一级栏目 状态 现在能读什么 能力地图 成熟 能力拓扑、价值与成熟度、自评矩阵——本站的判断框架 系统设计 成熟（旗舰） AWS EKS 生产级高可用集群标准是全站质量基准；另有 5 篇 Kubernetes 生产标准 变更交付 参考手册 Argo CD GitOps、Jenkins→K8s 流水线、部署回滚 Runbook 可靠运行 参考手册 6 篇可观测性标准 + 4 篇恢复 Runbook（PVC、磁盘扩容、证书过期、健康检查） 平台治理 参考手册 GitLab、JumpServer 堡垒机、发布安全检查清单、Redash 案例复盘 待补旗舰 已有 3 篇匿名迁移案例；深度剖析与决策复盘的旗舰文章建设中 标准手册 成熟（最厚） 约 30 篇按技术域组织的运维标准 + 文档模板，可直接查

docs.title: 最值得先读的三条路径
docs.text:
1. 想判断这站够不够硬：直接读 AWS EKS 生产级高可用集群标准，它是全站的质量基准。
  2. 想给自己定位：读自评与成长路径，用六维度矩阵打分。
  3. 想查具体工具的标准做法：去标准手册，按技术域或标签检索。

待补旗舰的栏目不会用空写作规范页浪费你的时间——它们明确标注“建设中”，并链向最近的实体内容。

docs.title: 价值与成熟度
docs.text:
定义：DevOps 是一种工程化协作模式，用自动化、标准化、可观测性和反馈机制，把“开发交付”与“生产运行”连接成一个持续改进系统。

它的价值不在于“用了多少工具”，而在于是否稳定改善下面这条闭环：

flowchart LR A["更小批量变更"] --> B["更快交付"] B --> C["更低失败率"] C --> D["更快恢复"] D --> E["更可信的业务节奏"] E --> A 价值维度 低成熟度表现 高成熟度表现 可量化指标 交付速度 手工发布、靠人记步骤 Pipeline 标准化、环境晋级清晰 部署频率、交付周期 变更安全 发布靠经验，失败后临场处理 灰度、回滚、变更冻结、发布门禁 变更失败率、回滚耗时 恢复能力 故障靠个人英雄主义 有 SLO、告警分级、演练和复盘 MTTR、故障影响时长 系统透明度 有监控但定位慢 指标、日志、链路和业务上下文联动 MTTD、告警有效率 成本效率 资源长期闲置，无归属 容量模型、成本归因、弹性策略 单位业务成本、闲置资源比例 安全治理 凭证散落、上线后补扫描 Secrets、审计、供应链、安全门禁前置 高危漏洞修复周期、权限漂移数量

docs.title: 成熟度五级
docs.text:
级别 状态 特征 最大风险 L1 工具化 有脚本和工具 能减少重复操作，但依赖个人 自动化了错误流程 L2 流程化 有标准流程 发布、回滚、审批、排障开始可复用 流程存在，但执行质量不稳定 L3 平台化 有共享能力 多团队能用统一模板和平台交付 平台变成新的工单入口 L4 度量化 有工程指标 能用数据评估交付、可靠性、成本和安全 只看指标，不看真实风险 L5 自适应 有反馈闭环 事故、成本、交付数据会反向改进平台和流程 组织复杂度上升，需要治理能力

真正的 DevOps 成熟度不是“有没有平台”，而是“平台、流程和团队行为是否持续降低生产风险”。

判断自己的位置后，可以用自评与成长路径给自己打分。

docs.title: 自评与成长路径
docs.text: 先区分角色边界：不是所有运维工作都叫 DevOps，也不是所有 DevOps 都必须写平台。关键在于你是否能把“系统运行”与“变更交付”连接起来，并用工程方法持续改进。

docs.title: 水平分层
docs.text: 水平 常见状态 能独立完成什么 还缺什么 不属于 DevOps 只接触应用使用或简单部署 按文档操作少量工具 缺系统、网络、发布、故障处理基础 未达 Ops 会执行命令，但不知道影响面 重启服务、复制文件、看简单日志 缺 Linux、网络、权限、备份恢复能力 Ops 能维护生产系统 主机、服务、日志、备份、常规故障处理 缺交付自动化、平台化、可靠性设计 初级 DevOps 能把重复操作脚本化 基础 CI/CD、容器构建、简单部署 缺生产风险意识和系统性回滚设计 中级 DevOps 能负责一条交付链路 Pipeline、Kubernetes 部署、监控告警、常规排障 缺跨团队标准化、容量、安全、成本治理 高级 DevOps 能设计生产级方案 高可用架构、灰度发布、SLO、事故复盘、平台模板 缺组织级架构取舍和长期演进能力 DevOps 架构师 能定义多团队标准 平台架构、治理模型、黄金路径、迁移策略 需要更强业务约束、成本和组织影响判断 DevOps 专家 能处理复杂系统与组织问题 识别错误路线、建立反馈闭环、用复盘推动体系升级 风险是脱离一线细节，变成只讲原则

docs.title: 自评分矩阵
docs.text:
给每个维度打 0 到 4 分：

  • 0：不了解，无法独立判断。
  • 1：能按文档操作。
  • 2：能独立交付常规任务。
  • 3：能设计方案并处理异常。
  • 4：能定义标准、指导他人，并用数据持续改进。
维度 0-1 分表现 2 分表现 3-4 分表现 Linux / 网络 / 存储 只会常用命令 能排查常见系统问题 能做容量、隔离、恢复和安全基线 容器 / Kubernetes / 云 会部署示例 能维护常规集群和服务 能设计生产级高可用、升级和故障恢复 CI/CD / 制品 / 环境 能跑流水线 能维护发布流程 能设计安全、可回滚、可审计的交付体系 可观测性 / 故障处理 会看日志和图表 能定位常见故障 能设计告警分级、SLO 和复盘闭环 安全 / 合规 / 成本 知道基本概念 能执行扫描和权限配置 能把治理嵌入平台与交付流程 平台化 / 标准化 / 影响力 只服务自己 能沉淀脚本和模板 能服务多团队并量化平台价值

docs.title: 分数解释
docs.text:
总分 判断 建议 0-6 未达 Ops 或刚入门 先补 Linux、网络、权限、日志、备份和故障处理 7-12 Ops 到初级 DevOps 把重复操作脚本化，建立基础 CI/CD 和容器化能力 13-18 中级 DevOps 重点补可靠性、回滚、可观测性和安全前置 19-22 高级 DevOps 开始做跨团队标准、平台抽象、容量和成本治理 23-24 架构师 / 专家候选 用真实案例、事故复盘和指标证明体系性影响

判断自己是否在进步，不要只看“学了几个工具”。更硬的证据是：你是否让发布更安全、恢复更快、故障更少、成本更清楚、团队更少依赖个人经验。

知道自己在哪一档后，用内容成熟度地图找到下一步该读的内容。

docs.title: DevOps 能力拓扑
docs.text:
DevOps 能力不是工具清单，而是从基础设施到组织反馈的多层能力栈。工具只是实现手段，真正有价值的是每一层能否产生可验证的工程结果。

flowchart TB A["基础层<br/>Linux / 网络 / 存储 / 安全基线"] --> B["运行层<br/>容器 / Kubernetes / 云资源 / 中间件"] B --> C["交付层<br/>Git / CI / CD / 制品 / 环境治理"] C --> D["可靠性层<br/>SLO / 容量 / 变更风险 / 灾备 / 回滚"] D --> E["可观测层<br/>指标 / 日志 / 链路 / 告警 / 复盘"] E --> F["平台层<br/>自助化 / 标准化 / 多团队复用 / 成本治理"] F --> G["组织反馈层<br/>度量 / 责任边界 / 决策复盘 / 工程文化"] A -.支撑.-> D C -.约束.-> D E -.反馈.-> C G -.改进.-> F 能力域 核心问题 典型产物 能力证据 基础设施 系统是否可安装、可加固、可恢复？ 基线镜像、网络规划、存储策略、访问控制 能解释一次主机或网络故障如何定位和恢复 容器与云原生 服务是否可移植、可调度、可扩缩？ Dockerfile、Helm Chart、Kubernetes 集群、云资源模块 能设计生产级发布、扩容、回滚和隔离策略 交付工程 变更是否可重复、可审计、可回滚？ CI/CD Pipeline、制品仓库、环境 promotion、发布门禁 能降低发布失败率，而不是只把命令自动化 可靠性工程 系统失败时是否有边界、有预案、有恢复路径？ SLO、容量模型、降级方案、故障演练、复盘机制 能把事故转化为结构性改进 可观测性 出问题时能否快速定位，而不是只有“监控”？ 指标、日志、链路、告警分级、值班面板 能减少噪音告警，提高定位速度 平台工程 多团队是否能用同一套标准交付和运维？ 自助平台、黄金路径、模板、权限模型、成本报表 能让团队少找人、多自助、少犯错 安全与治理 速度是否建立在可控风险上？ Secrets 管理、审计、供应链扫描、最小权限、合规检查 能把安全前置到交付流程，而不是上线前补锅

一个可靠的 DevOps 工程师不一定每层都最强，但必须知道每层之间的依赖关系。只会工具、不懂交付风险；只会交付、不懂生产可靠性；只会平台、不懂团队反馈，都会在生产环境里暴露短板。

这张拓扑也是本站导航的来源：系统设计对应基础层与运行层，变更交付对应交付层，可靠运行对应可靠性层与可观测层，平台治理对应平台层与组织反馈层。

docs.title: 案例复盘
docs.text:
案例复盘负责证明经验。深度剖析、实战案例、决策复盘和事故复盘里，记录的是只有真干过的人才写得出的判断。

这一类内容比成功故事更能证明经验。它保留规模、约束、症状、权衡和结果，但移除身份信息。

docs.title: 实战案例
docs.text:
• 匿名 Kubernetes 升级案例
  • 匿名对象存储迁移案例
  • 匿名应用容器化迁移案例

docs.title: 匿名化边界
docs.text:
• 可保留：服务规模、节点数量范围、故障症状、迁移阶段、约束、决策原因和结果范围。
  • 必须移除：客户名、公司名、真实域名、账号、密钥、私有仓库、内部 IP、个人数据和未公开业务细节。

docs.title: 待补
docs.text:
• 深度剖析：为什么高可用不是多节点、为什么发布系统最难的是回滚、为什么告警越多越不可靠。
  • 决策复盘：一个错误技术决策如何被发现和纠正。
  • 事故复盘：时间线、现象 vs 根因、当时的误判、结构性改动。

这些旗舰文章建设中，将在有足够真实、脱敏的素材后写出。详见内容成熟度地图。

docs.title: 变更交付
docs.text:
变更交付关注一件事：变更如何安全、可审计、可回滚地进入生产。

交付文章必须解释发布前检查、发布中观察、失败判据、回滚触发条件和权限边界。把命令自动化只是起点，真正的目标是让发布失败率下降。

docs.title: CI/CD 与 GitOps
docs.text:

  • Jenkins 到 Kubernetes 流水线标准

docs.title: 回滚设计
docs.text: • 部署回滚 Runbook —— 回滚不只是一个 Runbook，它是发布架构的一部分。

docs.title: 待补
docs.text: 发布策略（灰度、发布门禁、变更冻结、变更失败率）是当前的重要内容缺口，将作为深度文章补齐。详见内容成熟度地图。

docs.title: 平台治理
docs.text:
平台工程把重复的交付、运维、安全和治理工作，变成可复用的平台能力。它不是给脚本套一个界面。

平台文章必须区分平台能力和平台界面。如果一个功能不能减少认知负担、重复风险或反馈时间，它可能不值得平台化。

安全也不是孤立的检查清单，而是平台和交付体系的一部分：最小权限、凭证生命周期、供应链风险、审计和变更控制都应嵌入 DevOps 工作流。

docs.title: 可靠运行
docs.text:
可靠运行不是一堆监控工具的集合。它通过目标、信号、响应、演练和复盘来管理系统失败。

可观测性的核心观点不是“有没有监控”，而是“系统能不能说清自己怎么坏的”。指标、日志、链路和事件应该服务于定位，而不是堆面板数量。

docs.title: 标准手册
docs.text:
标准和模板是生产工程的基础设施。它们减少重复犯错，但不替代深度文章或案例复盘。

这里按技术域组织所有运维标准，是查询入口，不是站点的概念主线。你来这里是要答案——查命令、标准、模板、Runbook——不关心它叫 standard 还是 technical KB。

标准文章必须解释适用范围、强制项、推荐项、异常处理、验证方法和维护责任人。

docs.title: 系统设计
docs.text:
系统设计回答一个问题：一套系统能不能在真实流量、真实故障和真实组织约束下运行。

架构文章必须解释约束、备选方案、失败模式、验证和回滚。一张组件图远远不够。
